subject: LIGO/Virgo S191124be: Global MASTER-Net observations report
submitter: Vladimir Lipunov at Moscow State U/Krylov Obs  <lipunov@xray.sai.msu.ru>
body:
V. Lipunov, E. Gorbovskoy, V.Kornilov, N.Tyurina, P.Balanutsa, A.Kuznetsov, F.Balakin, 
V.Vladimirov, D. Vlasenko, I.Gorbunov, D.Zimnukhov, V.Senik, T.Pogrosheva, D.Kuvshinov 
(Lomonosov Moscow State University, SAI, Physics Department),

R. Podesta, C.Lopez, F. Podesta, C.Francile 
(Observatorio Astronomico Felix Aguilar OAFA),

H.Levato 
(Instituto de Ciencias Astronomicas, de la Tierra y del Espacio ICATE),

R. Rebolo, M. Serra 
(The Instituto de Astrofisica de Canarias),

D. Buckley 
(South African Astronomical Observatory),

O.A. Gres, N.M. Budnev, O.Ershova 
(Irkutsk State University, API),

A. Tlatov, D. Dormidontov 
(Kislovodsk Solar Station of the Pulkovo Observatory),

V. Yurkov, A. Gabovich, Yu. Sergienko 
(Blagoveschensk Educational State University)




MASTER-Tunka robotic telescope  (Global MASTER-Net: http://observ.pereplet.ru, Lipunov et al., 2010, Advances in Astronomy, vol. 2010, 30L)  located in Russia (Applied Physics Institute, Irkutsk State University) started inspect of the LIGO/Virgo S191124be errorbox  1625 sec after trigger time at 2019-11-24 10:26:23 UT, with upper limit up to  18.5 mag. Observations started at twilight.  The observations began at zenith distance = 42 deg. The sun  altitude  is -11.7 deg. 

MASTER-Amur robotic telescope  located in Russia (Blagoveshchensk State Pedagogical University) started inspect of the LIGO/Virgo S191124be errorbox  1630 sec after trigger time at 2019-11-24 10:26:28 UT, with upper limit up to  18.0 mag. The observations began at zenith distance = 57 deg. The sun  altitude  is -26.5 deg. 

The galactic latitude b = 38 deg., longitude l = 76 deg.


Real time updated cover map and OT discovered available here: 
https://master.sai.msu.ru/site/master2/ligo_1.php?id=10937

We obtain a following upper limits.

    2114 | 2019-11-24 10:33:02 |        MASTER-Tunka | (16h 57m 59.03s , +49d 38m 28.7s) |  P| |   180 | 18.2 |

Filter C is a clear (unfiltred) band. 


The observation and reduction will continue. 
The message may be cited.
